channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Law of the jungle — 126 replies — 463.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
Law of the jungle

Open thread • Replies: 126 • Updated: 2026-06-15 01:46 UTC

OP

>I am a million billion trillionair-
>BAM BAM BAM
>STAB STAB STAB
W-WHY AM I DYING I AM RICH AND HAVE MONEY ROCKETS CARS AIEEEEEE
P-PLEASE SIRE SAVE M-
>BAM BAM BAM
NOOOO WHY IS MONEY USELESS WHY WHY WHY WAAAAH PLEASE SIR
I WILL GIVE YOU EVERYTHIN-
>STAB HIS KIDS
AAAAAAH MY KIDDIES MY BLOODLINE WHY WHY
PLEASE SIR THE TRILLION MONEY
ACK

Early replies (first 3)

62359354
>>62359336
a mentally buck busted normie is going to say """the law""" grants the rich physical immunity.... and even if it doesn't, they have like, body guards who are omnipotent, omniscient, and omnipresent. you can't do ANYTHING to the rich!!!! it's literally impossible!!!!!

62359360
>>62359336
We live in capitalism and I'm protected by the pigs u need to learn how to move nigga

62359387
>nigger tier envy thread
>/biz/

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62365722
>>62365719
lumpen are more based than working class at least half the time

62365724
>>62359336
>I am an anon-
>KNOCK KNOCK KNOCK
W-WHY AM I GETTING ARRESTED I JUST MADE LE FUNNY POS-
>OPEN UP
NOOOOOOO IT WAS JUST A JOKE

62365730
>>62365722
Also in this shithole country labor isn't even worth it compared to gibs so I'd rather leech welfare and commit crimes than slave for poverty wages. I'm doing my part demoralizing these pathetic rightoid hard workers. The government pays for everything, gave me a huge house and I break the law almost daily KEK
I have everything my neighbours have

62365739
>>62359336
Yeah, but you won't do shit, though.

>>62359400
>t.never heard of drones

62365752
>>62365730
american lumpen do basically the same thing. american working poor also espouse righttarded views that further enrich their exploiters, retire into poverty, then cry bloody murder when anyone threatens their state funded retirement gibs like medicare/medicaid.

62365755
>>62365752
More like leisure class of the sewers
The only "negative" is that these working poor look down on me, but little do they know

62365772
The only respectable working class are the Marxists. Until the day you dare raise the red banners I'll keep on eating for you
At least I take the time to post agitprop inbetween gooning and smoking meth so you could say I engage in class struggle myself

62365810
>>62359336
The most based thread on /biz/

62365816
>>62365810
Meanwhile I got banned for posting a similar such thread the other day. Mods = fags
But lets keep it up comrades

62365827
>law of the jungle

Do you realize a man with a trillion dollars could hire 100,000 men to torture and rape you to death? lmao

62365831
>>62365827
that doesn't mean anything.

62365833
>>62365827
Yet he'd lose against a single methed out homeless nigger in psychosis
Curious no?

62365844
He will use his AI to imprison and torture you for writing this, jealous tard. You will be harmed by an AI forever.

62365846
>>62365844
is this the new striver cope?

62365866
>>62365659
this guy is either a larper or extremely lost.
>>/r/lgbt/
>>62365724
don't live in the UK

62365875
>>62365866
>4chan is my heckin super sekrit nazi hideout
Stfu faggot I've been here longer than you have
I used to be a capitalist cuck like most of you but something finally snapped recently. And I believe many on /biz/ are ready for a communist revolution having witnessed the capitalist system up close

62365880
>>62365827
The USA couldn't defeat Iran with a trillion dollars military
Russia couldn't defeat Ukraine with thousands of men. Ukraine can't defeat Russia with all the help they got from NATO nations

62366055
>>62365875
>I believe many on /biz/ are ready for a communist revolution
that's why /biz/ shits on communism so much.
>I used to be a capitalist cuck like most of you but something finally snapped recently.
you mean you're not making money and are too broke, in other words, stupid, to live comfortably in the system?
only poors want a redistribution of wealth.
>Stfu faggot I've been here longer than you have
No one cares. This is a crypto board and you're /pol/ posting.

62366077
>>62359336
why are you seething over someone else's wealth? someone that you don't even know? try it and you either end up dead or in prison either way you still lose.

62366107
>>62366055
materially I am personally comfortable though
But I want it all because I'm not a cuck like you

62366123
>>62365833

No he wouldn't. Power has never been individual and has always been about your ability to build organizations and leverage armies of men, dumbfuck. Can't even edgelord right without sounding like a toddler.

62366125
>Create strife and problems
>Create poor people by infiltrating government and taxing everyone to death and beyond
>"Gommunism will save you!"

62366130
>>62365880

We weren't talking about that, we were basing it off of your retarded little "but I could shoot him" hypothetical. If we're talking straight gang laws Elon Musk is still able to conquer entire micronations, much less have to deal with you lmfao.

That's actually one thing that's always vexed me, how cucked and servile billionaires must be to not leverage their astronomic wealth more in violent ways. Because they can afford to disappear anyone they want.

62366171
>>62366123
>Power has never been individua
BANG BANG BANG
>ACK WHY AM I DYING!
>I AM SUPER NEXT LEVEL RICH WITH CONTROL OF MY DADDIES BANKS AND THE COUNTRY
>I HAVE BILLIONS OF DOLLARS AND HUGE ARMIES OF SECURITY AND CARS
>THEY KILLED ME I WANT JUSTICE!
news says he dies of heart attack and case is closed
>NO NO NO THEY KILLED ME IT WASN'T A HEART ATTACK PLEASE
BANG BANG BANG
>AIIEEEEE MY MONEY TAKE IT!
dies

62366359
>>62366171
we'll smash blokhins record, comrade!

62366421
>>62366107
>I want it all
ok goy. you first. go rob a CEO or whatever dumb fuck move you think is going to make your life great and not just burn everything to the ground.
The reality is you have no power to change your situation.

62366441
>>62366421
Patiently biding my time until the day I may throw my red-hot glowing piece of coal into the palace of Capitalism

62366449
>>62366441
so you're planning on being an economic failure?
If you were able to become rich, you wouldn't want to burn things down.
If you weren't able to become rich, you're too stupid to become rich after a successful revolution.
BTW, pro tip: a successful revolution for retards like you is impossible.

62366459
>>62366449
Don't put words in my mouth you capitalist swine

62366543
>>62366449
how much do make a year?

item.title: The person who owns this isn't "on vacation." He does whatever he wants whenever he wants. — 62 replies — 228.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
The person who owns this isn't "on vacation." He does whatever he wants whenever he wants.

Open thread • Replies: 62 • Updated: 2026-06-15 01:46 UTC

OP

The person who owns this isn't "on vacation." He does whatever he wants whenever he wants. He's upper class. And has 3 passports and owns properties and businesses (wealth) around the world. He isn't waiting on a paycheck to drive down to Costco.
Hint: it's a Rothschild property.
Making even a million bucks a year writing code for Elon or Bezos or Brin does NOT put you in the upper class. At all.
I am European and only we have class. We invented class.

Early replies (first 3)

62356062
>>62356048
Before AI, you could write code for ten years and then buy this.

62356086
Got so mad in the other thread you thought you would start a new thread about it LMAO

62356191
this is that same polish guy who was seething in the other thread about class or whatever

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62360058
>>62359212
>>62359283
"The fool says in his heart that there is no God"

Maybe try turning away your sinful ways, repenting and searching scripture instead of looking for handouts. Or you could just live out your days as a hard hearted rancid transgressor and go to the grave an unbelieving fool. Nothing anyone says can change your blackened heart. The Holy Spirit does that work.

62360102
>>62360058
why is a 24 year old mulatto born again christian zoomer trying to give me life advice?

62360116
>>62359522
you have free will to do what you want. But be warned that you lash out at the unblemished Lamb of God. The Alpha and the Omega who healed every infirmity and cast out demons from the land. The Good Shepherd who has defeated and and is Seated at the right hand of Power. The sad thing about people like you is that you are so fallen and your nature is so corrupt that you cannot even hear the Truth if it was read to you. Even the demons themselves that Jesus drove out of people KNEW that he was the Son of the Most High. Repent brother. The Kingdom of Heaven is at hand. We know not the day nor the hour of His return

62360814
>>62359005
>these death monuments will be renouned and respected and not some dinky little tourist hotsp-ACK

62361163
>>62357608
Why do people hate on these posts instead of try to figure out ways to improve their quality of life to have similar?

That's the whole point of this board. Instead of hating on people showing off their wealth figure out how to get it too.

I thought it was only like this on hip hop forums. Everyone hat s on each other and then refuse all ways of getting to it.

62361183
>>62359210
Or stop all or nothing thinking and find a way to live well so you can have wealth and not have to compromise yourself in any way?

62362527
>>62356048
Why would I want such a monstrosity.

62362541
>>62360116
>unfathomably based life speaking minister of /biz
I will miss you when the tranny kike mods ban you for speaking truth, but I know you will bear your cross with dignity.

62362654
>>62356048
why would you even want this? what are you going to use it for? throw parties 24/7 for people you fucking hate? If you live their 24/7 the nice view and garden just become mundane like everything else only now you have a lot less money

62362675
>>62362527
You’re not European, you would not get it.

62362885
>>62358695
>stay poor and let the Jews control wealth and power like a good goy

62363916
>>62356048
everyone there is a pedophile and you'd be asked to leave if you said nigger or faggot. pass.

62363922
>>62356062
Is becoming an expert in AI still a way to make money at all?

62364033
>>62362527
Nothing that a lick of paint, some landscaping and interior decorating couldn't fix. Location, location, location.

62364034
>>62356048
looks boring and not very comfy

62364139
>>62359212
Perhaps we are in hell.

62364148
>>62356048
>We invented class.
But you're not in it.

62364174
>>62356048
That’s nice buddy

62364781
>>62356048
Yeah and when the chips were on the ground and it came to violence the European elite were pretty much powerless to stop the germans twice.

Rich and WEAK.

meanwhile it was the poor, but self sustainable and smart North American freemen that had to save you TWICE.

Stop idolizing the rich and upper class. They are garbage retards, their only contribution is capital, but is capital really just ones and zeros on a hard drive, is that really what drives the will and spirit of a nation? The rich are retards.

62364797
>>62356048
I am the CEO of a very major circumcision equipment company and I endorse this message.

62364997
>>62357456
Bet he watches MTV's cribs all day. And thinks posh tacky is better than burger punk tacky. Lots of rich people do not live beyond their means to show off. It can even be hard to tell they are rich. It is the obscenely old money rich and new money niggers who have those villas. Or giant ye-haw ranch style estates you find distasteful.

62365139
>>62358413
What store do you run?

62365155
>>62356048
Rothchilds don’t have a dollar amount worth like we think, the Rothchilds simply have infinite power to do as they want, the entire world’s economic output is in their hands and they have the connections with every elite of whom have connections with all the powerful people to get anything done they desire. They just can’t do extreme projects where they have like a million slaves to build them a mansion on the moon, without causing a goyim uprising, that’s the endgame of the Jews, to have the ability to truly freely do anything they want with zero resistance.

62365166
Europeans also cucked nobility with the Hanseatic League and private banking families.

62365170
>>62363922
Ai needs arms and legs

62365198
>>62365155
Goyim are annoying.

62365372
>>62358695
With they way you are talking you are lukewarm, you should repent.

>Muh Jesus Christ but I curse and talk hatered.
You should act good first other wise your believe in Jesus Christ are fake and Worth nothing. Acting from love = Jesus Christ. Meanwhile you posted hatred and curses.

62365921
>>62358695
>meditate of the words of Jesus Christ
> There is neither Jew nor Greek, slave nor free, male nor female, for you are all one in Christ Jesus.
> You worship what you do not know; we worship what we do know, for salvation is from the Jews.

Cognitive dissonant christcuck faggot who thinks his religion is based and redpilled because can’t cope with the fact it is Jewish slop because he made it his whole identity and his ego too fragile to accept how wrong it is

62365927
>>62365139
Can't say but it's not the usual 'dropship on le amazon' type stuff you hear. There are niches out there to find

62366101
>>62356048
ok, and? is that your place?

item.title: No regrets selling BTC?? — 98 replies — 196.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
No regrets selling BTC??

Open thread • Replies: 98 • Updated: 2026-06-15 01:33 UTC

OP

Some talk about regretting it.

Anyone sold and still thinks it was the right move?

If you had the cash from that sale sitting in your account today, would you buy BTC again? Because I definitely would.

Early replies (first 3)

62339349
I sold my last bitcoin last year. Shitcoin is dead. AI is dead. Time to start acting like an african american.

62339372
>>62339349
Maybe regrets for buying? Should have bought chainlink

62339382
I rotated into XMR at like $105k, no regrets at all.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62354827
>>62339328
Anyone bullish on avalanche?

62355050
>>62339328
I sold when the Epstein files dropped. Feels good.

62355133
>>62354803
That’s the part a lot of people overlook. If TBVs actually work as intended, I think people are underestimating how much TVL native BTC use cases could pull in.

62355136
>>62355050
Time will tell whether it was the right call, but at least you acted according to your move.

62355617
>>62355133
It's crazy that DeFi security is still one of the biggest problems in the space.

62355631
>>62354827
Buy avax today and sell @ $100

62355632
>>62339417
Monero programmers, like the telegram founder, can be compromised
And monero is based on bitcoin. The entire crypto market exists because of all the money in bitcoin which is now backed by the US government.

62356269
>>62355617
At this point, thorough audits and multiple layers of review should be the standard, not a nice bonus anon.

62356538
>>62355631
What's your basis?

62357046
>>62345600
Probably the better route. We've seen too many centralized services blow up over the years, so you can't really blame people for preferring fully decentralized setups.

62357057
>>62355632
What about ZEC?

62357077
ICP

62357115
>>62339328
I sold at 116k I dont regret it at all and have about 2 mil waiting for sept-dec buy in.

62357126
>>62339328
I can never regret buying BTC desu, I bought back in around 70K and will DCA more if price goes below 60K. I'm also staking a little BTC on babylon for some extra yield

62357923
>>62355632
>Monero programmers, like the telegram founder, can be compromised
yeah just like the bitcoin developers right? they're the ones who control bitcoin?

it's a good question though, what if they were? miners would have to agree on consensus on which code to accept. if github ever refused to host monero's code then it would just move to .onion or .i2p

62359525
>>62357046
That's how it goes. They've already got GoMining, Ledger, and a16z involved, so let's see who they bring in next.

62359541
>>62357057
ZEC is interesting. If the privacy narrative picks up again, it's usually one of the first names people bring up.

62359557
>>62339328
I literally don't care about it. Mogged by virtually everything else over the last couple of years.

Also why is faggot shit like dogecoin still worth billions of dollars.

>in b4 bottom signal
Probably.

62359588
>>62357126
Sounds like you've got a plan and you're sticking to it. Hard to ask for much more than that. I wouldn't be surprised if more people follow suit over time. The $4B+ TVL shows there's already plenty of interest.

62359608
>>62359557
The market has a weird habit of rewarding the things nobody can rationally explain.

62359673
>>62357077
People still remember the launch, but the tech discussion around ICP is often more interesting than the price chart.

62360479
>>62359557
When bottom

62361782
>>62360479
Nobody knows

62362657
>>62359557
How do you mean?

62362661
>>62339328
While BTC is in deep bear stage, it's time to get in. I need to be greedy when others are fear.

62363445
>>62362657
It's his opinion

62364599
>>62339349
>Time to start acting like an african american.
finally someone said it

62365449
>>62362661
You're just doing the right thing..

62365460
any time you can cash out, turn a profit and not get anally blasted by exchanges is a good time to cash out.

62368338
>>62364599
Damn

item.title: Anon, someone oneshotted a Runescape-quality MMO with Claude. — 62 replies — 44.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Anon, someone oneshotted a Runescape-quality MMO with Claude.

Open thread • Replies: 62 • Updated: 2026-06-15 00:40 UTC

OP

Anon, someone oneshotted a Runescape-quality MMO with Claude. If you're not able to get filthy rich with this tech, you are just a brainless clown.

https://worldofclaudecraft.com/

Early replies (first 3)

62362742
oldschool runescape is free

62362747
looks like shit
>If you're not able to get filthy rich with this tech, you are just a brainless clown.
sure if you can scam some subhumans but you're competing with 1 billion jeet scammers

62362749
>>62362737
Buy
An
Ad
!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62364348
>>62364090
When are you retards going to learn? LLMs learn in almost the same way humans do. They understand things conceptually.

62364357
People with balls played this MMO

62364390
>>62363118
it's not automatic, there's literally a mod that goes through the chat logs and bans anyone who says nigger

62364391
>>62362753
So you get to be a funko pop?

62364400
sounds gay

62364408
>>62364390
What's he get paid?

62364447
>>62362737
IMAGINE OSRS 2.0 holy

62364461
>>62362843
>>62362844
it's so over for snailcat retards

62364719
>>62362737
impressive but 7 fps isn't going to work.

62364883
>>62364719
>7FPS isn't going to work
I bet you dont have hardware acceleration on dumbie

62364978
>>62363623
the assets are free open source models and stuff so it looks like shit

The gameplay is a classic wow clone, it's shockingly similar

62365021
>>62364090
Thanks...these dumbfucks thinks it cool they reskinned something and made it gay

62365211
>>62362737
>oneshotted
100% guarantee it wasn't actually "oneshotted". Also Fable 5 got banned already, even if you could one shot, it's over now.

62365245
>>62362737
this is trash

62365483
>>62362737
Dune banned AI for a reason.

62365496
>>62365211
/loop until complete

62365514
https://github.com/levy-street/world-of-claudecraft/blob/main/src/game/interactions.ts

>if if else if if else if if else else if if if else if

it's claude code alright

62365881
>>62362737
>Runescape-quality
so it's utter unplayable dogshit?
wow AI is the future

62365955
>>62362737
>>62362753
>>62363118
Globo-Homo Of SlopCraft

62365991
>>62362737
Very interesting. Not the project though, any starter $10 RPG template is a more complete game with less bugs. It's a completely useless code base worth $0. It's only impressive to people who understand nothing about game development. But it's interesting that OP hired spam bots for promotion. Is he somehow making money off of it?

62366047
>>62362742
>>62363625
in the shitty art style
also runescape is ultrashit skinner box

62366586
>>62364357
FUCK YEAH UTANI NIGGER

62366746
>>62362737
If you rip an existing open source game yourself rather than ask Claude to do it, you get documentation to go along with it.

62366802
>>62362737
Claude made me a trading bot for LINK. I am now down 99%. Thanks, claude!

62366858
>>62366802

perfect, 2 mistakes in one go!
>/biz/ dream

62367257
>>62366746
Or you just ask claude to create documentation along with the code and you don't get in trouble with the original creators.

62367267
>>62362775
It's so funny to see all the people boasting 10000x productivity and yet nothing changed. Actually everything is worsening, again and again.

62368083
>>62362737
hard pass.

62368165
If you pay attention to this guys comment
>>62363356

Tested it. My machine is not too powerful,
and was able to make some few quests.

For me it really feels like this comment: >>62364978
>The gameplay is a classic wow clone, it's shockingly similar
I'm a bit impressed! If he did not have to do a lot by himself,
imagine how much you can achieve by what little effort!!!

>This was made in 2 days and 91% of the Max 20x plan,
>as the author stated on the Reddit thread,
>so roughly ~$200.
>Supposedly, existing free assets were used and weren't generated.

Found a youtube video, where the game runs smoothly!
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=cNzI3Ll2lHA

62368174
>>62368083
I would argue because the strafe on Q and E, and you could always change it if you wanted.

item.title: The business model of AI is getting a little funky.. — 10 replies — 40.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
The business model of AI is getting a little funky..

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-06-15 01:51 UTC

OP

The business model of AI is getting a little funky..
I don't think this is a one off, you are always gonna be able to jailbreak them, and with increasing potency, increasing concerns of "national security".
Maybe we get a handful of companies with security clearance to utilize them, but if we say Anthropic does a "medical research AI" , you are gonna end up with a couple of customers e.g. Pfizer, Eli Lilly.. If they are the sole customers, then it is a LOT they have to pay anthropic to build this AI.

Early replies (first 3)

62366740
>>62366701
So?

62366745
>>62366701
Collapse of empire is always so messy

62366773
This is just going to give extra incentive for the Chinese to expand into that vacuum with a knockoff version kek

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62366775
>>62366701
The "jailbreak" is blown way out of proportion. Any retard can get any ai to attempt to do "bad" stuff, only thing fable 5 had was speed and less need to error correct.

Probably boils down to the government wanting to set the world back another 10years for "national security" because they're afraid some goyium might stubble upon a AI assisted cure to cancer.
>BUT HAXKERS!!!!1!!!
>BUT BAD GOYYS!!!!1!!!!!1!!
Send another 20gorillianz to our greatest ally, i guess?

We're still in full globohomo mode and we can't have the masses solving problems without daddy government's approval.

62366781
>>62366773
>This is just going to give extra incentive for the Chinese to expand into that vacuum with a knockoff version kek
And this. Unironically pushing us behind.

62366784
>>62366775
Mode the leader of Anthropocene has been saying ai is dangerous and needs to be regulated for years, well he just got his wish haha

62366795
>>62366784
I hope a knockoff rolls out ASAP that we can run locally. This is retarded, the boomers ruining everything is getting tiresome.

62368323
From my POV, the fundamental issue is that the AI buildout is based on an idea of future profit.. And you can kind of see why/how with Anthropic, ~$30 billion in revenue, like people/companies are paying real money for AI.
5 years ago their revenue was nothing, this is sort of what the entire AI thing is about from a business perspective. Hard money is being made, and the growth rate is insane
..but now the growth rate isn't insane (?) because Antrophic can't release it's products. Like people have to pay for AI, they have to use AI, otherwise all these datacenters are ???

62368335
>>62366701
Isn't there a law that states the government has to buy the software for the cost of development plus a bit of profit?

62368403
>>62368335
Anthropic and OpenAI is kind of limited to the 1/4 of the world economy.. Prior to mythos, the pie they were gonna eat was 100%.
Rather than spreading out the cost of the datacenter build out, it will be concentrated amongst American companies and consumers..
I don't think the math for the AI valuation work any longer..

item.title: How do I fully Divest from the USA — 10 replies — 31.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
How do I fully Divest from the USA

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-06-15 01:44 UTC

OP

Anyone who’s still investing in U.S markets at this point is abetting fascism. I want a total European portfolio to support Liberal democracy

Early replies (first 3)

62368148
I unironically do this and it's easy.

Just a case of EU+UK tracker ETFs + Canada and Australia + Japan-Taiwan-SK. I balance it out with pan-European IT and defence ETFs to balance out the overabundance of financials and industrials.

This gives you a portfolio of liberal democracies.

62368154
>>62368132
>fascism

62368162
>>62368154
You’re just uneducated on history

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62368190
>>62368162
>If you don't let in a billion muzzies then you're fascist
You have to go back, Ismail.

62368216
>>62368190
>right wing dictatorial rule based on theocracy where people are rounded up in camps and killed isn’t fascism

62368221
>>62368132
haha thanks for the laughs

62368279
>>62368132
Anon, a lot of Europe is ran by kikes and pozzed with the same cultural wars as America, with the same objectives. Call it fascism if you want, or white genocide, or total podslavery, it doesn't matter.
The one thing neither Europe (except for Switzerland) nor the US are is democracies. You never vote policy, you vote representatives. And they do not represent you, they represent the elite.

62368283
>>62368132
Stop using money because all trade is denominated in dollars. Even the ones using some other meme store of value like gold or yuan. It's all dollars with more steps.

62368351
>>62368132

62368377

item.title: LINK is the textbook case of a great product bolted to a pointless token. — 8 replies — 6.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
LINK is the textbook case of a great product bolted to a pointless token.

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-06-15 00:49 UTC

OP

LINK is the textbook case of a great product bolted to a pointless token. The oracle network works, but using it never required buying LINK, so usage never bid up the price. Value accrues to Chainlink Labs, not to holders.

That’s the rot. A bloated org of hundreds of highly paid remote staff, funded the old fashioned way, by quietly selling treasury LINK into retail. You’re not an investor. You’re exit liquidity for payroll.

The bank narrative is years of pilots and press releases with almost no production revenue. SWIFT “integrations” don’t print fees. Meanwhile the flagship venues route around Chainlink entirely. Hyperliquid runs its own oracle, Polymarket settles via UMA, and Pyth has eaten the lucrative derivatives feeds.

Staking, payment abstraction, the “reserve”: bolt on mechanics invented years late to retrofit a reason to hold a token the network never actually needed. Hence the chart.

Early replies (first 3)

62368013
>>62368008
Ok but.... price?? Oh... and... chart?
Didn't read btw

62368019
Price. Chart. Now.

62368024
>>62368008
And the “staking” is theater. In real proof of stake, the people securing the network bond capital and lose it if they cheat. Chainlink staking does neither. The node operators actually running the data feeds aren’t required to stake a single LINK, so the stake secures nothing that matters. The feeds would run identically if the staking contract vanished tomorrow.

And the “yield” isn’t a cut of fees the network earns. It’s LINK paid out of a reserve the company pre allocated, your own token emissions handed back to you and relabeled as income. Strip the branding and it’s a lockup program that pays you in freshly minted allocation to sit still and shrink the float, so the chart looks less ugly than the selling deserves.

That isn’t staking. It’s a marketing line with an APR printed next to it.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62368056
peel check required. Input layer count here:______

62368059
>>62368024
If the nodes don't have to stake anything, doesn't that mean anyone could run a node and get subsidized by Sergey since it's decentralized? Oh no wait a sec, how would that even work? That seems like it would only be feasible is he was picking and choosing his buddies to actually run these, and at that point can you even call this a decentralized system? This seems like an... Odd... Structure.

62368185
Kek grok generate me another immaculate fud essay to dab on these stinky niggers

62368198
>>62368019
$8 kek
>how long you hodling that useless token

62368222
>>62368008
>>62368024
bro you just debunked your own dumb premisem token sneeded

item.title: Stocks that are expected to surge in the future are located in Japan. — 2 replies — 1.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Stocks that are expected to surge in the future are located in Japan.

Open thread • Replies: 2 • Updated: 2026-06-15 01:01 UTC

OP

Data Section, a Japanese company, has acquired a large number of Nvidia GPUs.
They will likely generate revenue by operating data centers based on these GPUs.
VLC Security, another Japanese company, is also involved in the operation of these data centers.
Considering their future earnings, both companies' stock prices are currently low.
Buying now would likely result in the stock price increasing many times over.
Data Section's stock code is 3905, and VLC Security's is 2467.

Early replies (first 3)

62366154
https://www.datasection.co.jp/en

https://www.datasection.co.jp/en-financial-report/en-ir-20260604001.pdf

Data Section and VLC Security are connected to Nvidia.

62368251
The stock prices of both Data Section and VLC Security have started to rise!
VLC Security HP
https://vlcsecurity.com/

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

item.title: how much money do u need to date women? Also are they ok if u are not ambitious career driven like if i have 10M but NEE… — 47 replies — 164.4/hr

Open thread • Replies: 47 • Updated: 2026-06-15 01:45 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62365550
dating is outdated chud, find something else to focus on

62365555
>>62365550
but i want a gf to watch mobis with, to eat some delicious food, cook together maybe and stuff.. I got a bluray player recently and im building up a collection we could do it together if i had a gf

:(

62365557
>>62365540
a good prostitute in euro cuck land will service you starting from 100$ for 30 mins. Anything else is cope

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62365738
>>62365587

- Looks = 1 - 10
- Wealth = 1 - 10
- Status = 1 - 10

- Personality = 1 - 10 (Low personality score = go-with-the-flow, outgoing, fun and risk-taking behavior. High personality score = standoffish, self-isolating, boring and risk-averse behavior.)

- Proximity = 1 - 10 (Low proximity score = living in a dense urban area and working in a high-volume traffic area. High proximity score = living in a rural area and working in a low-volume traffic area.)

- High-Value Male = 50+ Points
- Average Male = 10 - 25 Points
- Low-Value Male = 0 - 9 Points
- Incel = < 0 Points

62365748
>>62365540
Women want a 9+ handsome face, ripped bod and big dick. Money is cope.

62365871
>>62365540
>>62365555
You answered your own question. It's not about a specific threshold of money it's the perception you're working hard for HER specifically.

genuinely nice girls exist, but are extremely rare. if you want to NEET with someone for eternity unironically date an r9k foid

62365882
>>62365540
The biggest complaints from women are men who are mean, slobs and irresponsible. So if you have a bunch of money and want to NEET, it shouldn’t be that hard to sweep a fellow nerdy girl off her feet. Be affectionate, have a clean space, and don’t blow off things like a date night or blow through all your money lol
The girls who want grindset dudes are most likely to divorce rape you.

62365897
>>62365871
I think the problem is that nice girls are the hardest to find and not even that rare. I know several that still live at home to take care of their parents/family, and have at-home hobbies like knitting/crafting.
When the fuck would you meet any of them?? Especially if they work from home or a female dominated field like childcare.
My best advice will always be to make friends with people and then ask if they know any single women that would be a good match for you. You have to have the ability to show people you’re a good friend that wouldn’t fuck yup their friendship with the girl though

62365953
>>62365882

These are the complaints women have about the men they continue to date. So the stuff that is annoying but not a dealbreaker.

62365958

>have a dark and mysterious secret about yourself to keep her intrigued and excited (maybe you are the world’s best assassin, maybe you are an associate of a multi-billion dollar drug operation as a high ranking middleman, or you are a genius and secretly working on a project to save the energy crisis. 62366635

the world is full of poor women who will date you if you have a good personality

62367007

62367017
>>62366635

62367050
>>62367007
Yeah, that’s pretty retarded.
There are plenty of non-sexual reasons why a guy might know some other girl, like a family member, girlfriend/wife’s friend, former co-worker, former school friend.
You could make friends with girls to try to date one of their friends too.
The crux is on your ability to be a normal person to any normal person regardless of gender or age.
That’s how you find girls who aren’t sluts and divorce rapists. Not how you sleep around though.
So you have to figure out if what you really want is someone to have sex with regularly, or someone to start a family with. Because it’s a lot of social effort.

62367055
>>62367007

62367080

>>62365540

Don't just buy flowers, get them delivered to her work so all her coworkers get pissed and jealous. You want enough money to lock down a nice wedding and kids but thats really it, this was my fatal mistake. I have enough for three weddings now but opportunities are gone for me.

item.title: Why's he a trillionaire when his companies barely make money? — 100 replies — 30.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why's he a trillionaire when his companies barely make money?

Open thread • Replies: 100 • Updated: 2026-06-14 22:45 UTC

OP

>Tesla makes $3.9B/year, and the amount goes down year after year, yet has a 1.5T market cap
>SpaceX literally loses money and its claim to future profit is Grok, a cash-burning AI model that is losing to GPT and Claude
>Twitter is breakeven at best
What's going on here? Are white boomers just throwing money at him because they don't like seeing Indians, Chinese and Jews running shit in tech? This is the only explanation for the Elon Musk phenomenon I can make any sense of.

Early replies (first 3)

62361459
>>62361452
People rightly see him as a visionary for the future of humanity with the ability to actually do the stuf he foresees.

on the negative side much of what he envisions is distopian

and the first person to pioneer a technology is often NOT the person who gets rich off it

but you have to start somewhere, and he certainly does stuff other companies can't.

62361461
companies do not have to be profitable for people to speculate on them performing well in the future, and it helps when everyone said every company would go to zero but instead became some of the most valuable assets by marketcap in the world because they provided goods and services people wanted

62361471
>>62361459
>rightly
He hasn't even done anything, period.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62364330
>>62361452
Hypeman incarnated.

62364434
>>62364215
Sometimes I forget how retarded people on this board can be. Thanks for the reminder

62364440
>>62364434
It's genuinely fucked how antisemitic this board can be. Why Mossad hasn't gotten it shut down and its posters dragged through legal coals is fucking mystifying to me.

62364443
>>62364188
Yeah people don't seem to get that the whole point of the company is to offer a form of immortality down the road, by creating neural-digital interfacing that can bridge consciousness between brain and computer. Neuralink can and will charge any amount they want for that kind of tech, once it matures. They could price it at ten billion 2026 dollars per person and they would have a years or even decades-long waitlist.

62364868
>>62364443
yeah anon when moon

62364886
>>62361471

62364900
>>62363679

62364907

62364918
>>62361478

62365050
>>62361452
because stocks don't behave rationally and memes move the market more strongly than financial schizobabble

62365078
>>62361452

62365099
>>62361452
heres the thing. it was all vaporware the whole time...tesla included. it just wasnt as brazen as the ipo before...worse with every scam. so he could keep borrowing. because hes doing "buy borrow die", it became the banks problem at this point because hes never gonna pay them back, he couldnt if he wanted to, he couldnt if they tried to liquidate. they are heavily incentivized to help him dump the baggies on you to close out these transactions. then he can have a fake ketamine overdose and go party with jeffjeff again.

item.title: I work for an RIA managing assets and helping clients w/ financial planning AMAA >bought gold at $1900 >IBIT in my retir… — 37 replies — 30.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
I work for an RIA managing assets and helping clients w/ financial planning AMAA >bought gold at $1900 >IBIT in my retir…

Open thread • Replies: 37 • Updated: 2026-06-15 00:48 UTC

OP

I work for an RIA managing assets and helping clients w/ financial planning AMAA
>bought gold at $1900
>IBIT in my retirement accounts
>catastrophe bonds are my favorite asset class

Early replies (first 3)

62366984
>>62366943
i'm going DCA on 80% VTI 20% XSD until cows come home, and then exiting XSD entirely if the semiconductor sector goes completely tits-up
how does this risk profile compare to the recommended amount, assuming that i'm already tolerant of short-term drawdowns in favor of long term growth?

62367052
>>62366984
Depends on your time horizon, I'll assume you're young (<30) so a typical advisor would tell you that's an appropriate risk tolerance (100% us equity with an industry tilt). Most target date funds for a 2060 retirement are 90%+ equity right now.

The "rule of thumb" is 100 or 120 minus your age = how much % stocks you are overall.

Also, if you look into something called the "efficient frontier" the basic idea is the units of risk you take on per unit of return past 85% stocks is dog.

I'm worried about regime changes though, in which case stocks could have a decade+ of awful returns

62367065
>>62367052
>Also, if you look into something called the "efficient frontier" the basic idea is the units of risk you take on per unit of return past 85% stocks is dog.
5-15% bond ETF into the mix then? there's not much i can diversify into since my net worth is trash.

>I'm worried about regime changes though, in which case stocks could have a decade+ of awful returns
what would be the damage mitigation strategy for pure ETF portfolios? sell some (or all) and dip into bond ETFs?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62367144
>>62367052
Regime change is right, and gold to $20k+ and your clients will probably be itching to sue or worse that a rock ass stomped equities as equities enter a lost decade.

62367153
>>62367123
Actually the question I get almost weekly, most people are financially illiterate and only some realize it and try to change that. Good on ya.

Concepts to understand (off the top of my head in rough order of importance)
>ESTATE PLANNING SINCE YOU HAVE KIDS (do you have a trust/will?)
>Time value of money (how compounding returns get out of control over a long enough time span)
>Index funds and how few people beat the market (aka buy VT/VOO and BND and drool on yourself to beat 85% of asset managers)>Interest (what interest rates actually mean and what the risk free rate is)
>Budgeting/Cash Flows (how much are you saving and how much can you increase that through "offense" and "defense"
>Account tax types (IRA, 401k, normal brokerage, Roths, etc)
>4% safe withdrawal rate rule
>Why 10% (or higher) savings rate is important

The Simple Path to Wealth by JL Collins is a GREAT read if you have the time. Can be found online for free in PDF.

62367173
>>62367153
Many thanks for your time

62367184
>>62367144
Gold is real money, fiat is fiat.

Most people don't understand how our current system works and would be HORRIFIED if they know the shit that got pulled on them with SVB bank or that we print M2 into existence.

I made so much goddamn money when I saw them turn the printers on and bought GLD a few years ago.

>>62367141
That is incredibly risky, I'd ignore those calculators. Without seeing the data I can tell you they aren't looking back far enough for their calcs (30+ years, preferably 50). Maybe consider something that has these classes represented by ETF, example %s that I'd do:
60% VT
20% VGT
10% GLDM
10% BSV or ILS if you're spicy

This gives you both ex-US and US exposure with a heavy but prudent tech tilt on top of non-correlated assets to rebalance through volatility (gold and cat bonds). Cat bonds don't move with anything, they are truly uncorrelated. Gold goes up when equities go down usually, they like inflation and fear.

>>62367130
When do you plan to use the $?

62367187
>>62367173

62367230
>>62367213

> I have just been saving for shit like that, unexpected expenses
This is literally what insurance is for bro, 2% deductible and they buy you a new roof. I just went through that for hail damage.

How much does it cost a month all-in to exist?

62367266
>>62367052
>units of risk you take on per unit of return
One thing I've never understood from asset managers, everyone always says learn risk analysis, manage risk vs. reward, etc. I get it. But
HOW DO YOU MEASURE UNITS OF RISK AND RETURN????
Just give me some typical examples so I know what to look into. No one ever explains this.

62367326
>>62367184
>Maybe consider something that has these classes represented by ETF, example %s that I'd do:

>ILS
what is this and why does the graph sawtooth like that?

62367364
>>62367266
It's actually super easy, return is real return before taxes and "risk" is volatility aka standard deviation.

>>62367247
Bummer for that experience, but it could have been bad luck and it was a long time ago - regulations have grown stricter for insurance since then. Consider that your lived experience could be making you biased.

That being said, having a phat AF emergency fund can help you sleep at night, but 6 months is good and 12 months is phat and over 12 months is inefficient. Leads to why I asked about spending rate.

$1950/month x 130% because margin of safety and most people spend a little more on maintenance and shit than they think (property taxes?) = $~2,500/m so $30k a year living expenses.

You have over 4 years in what most would consider an efund man. It's pretty inefficient.

Think about meeting halfway and keeping $60k in the HYSA imo.

The rest can go into a taxable or even a Roth IRA if you AGI isn't over the MAGI threshold. Roths are VERY flexible on taking out contributions (any time, any reason) and give great tax advantages to the growth if you make it to 60.

The other option is a taxable brokerage (max flexibility, but no tax advantage). You could get over 3% pretty easily with minimal risk taken on.

50% VT, 20% BSV, 20% JPST, 10% ILS would give you such a nicer return with a hilariously low max drawdown. You could backtest it on portfolio visualizer to see, but would have to replace the ILS with BSV to get a decent backtest.

62367445
>>62367326
ILS is a catastrophe bond ETF. They buy all kinds of catastrophe bonds from around the world for different natural disasters. Catastrophe bonds are floating rate (no interest rate risk) bonds that only pay principal back if the named disaster doesn't occur.

Example: You can buy a Louisiana catastrophe bond from State Farm insurance and you don't get your principal back if a hurricane hits the state and it might pay something like 10% a year in interest. The insurance company is letting you "buy the risk".

ILS buys all kinds of cat bonds so is diversified enough that you don't get fucked. It's an investment category that as a result has NO CORRELATION to any other asset class. It doesn't move with anything which is amazing for portfolio building.

>why does the graph sawtooth like that?
That's the NAV going down proportionally to the fat af 1% a month dividend payments.

62367457
>>62367445
amazing. thanks for your insight and explanation.

62367509
>>62367457
Catastrophe bonds are a seriously unknown asset class for most people and absolutely incredible. Near equity returns with no correlation is insanity for fixed income. In 10 years or so they will be mainstream but until they the premium is nice to collect.

CBYYX for mutual funds and ILS for ETFs are what I target.

https://47598668.hs-sites.com/hubfs/ILS%20ETF%20Fund%20Documents/ILS%20ETF%20Fact%20Sheet.pdf?hsCtaAttrib=189598987686

62367655
>>62367364
>It's actually super easy, return is real return before taxes and "risk" is volatility aka standard deviation.
So you're saying for a stock to hit X price, the return = X - current price and risk is measured as number of standard deviations from current price to X?

62367658
>>62367445
>buy the risk
So it's like a bet. Isn't it exactly what crashed housing market in 2008?

62367683
>>62367655
Risk is defined as the uncertainty of future returns. It can be measure by volatility, or how wide the returns are historically. The statistical measure of this is standard deviation.

Ignore real returns for this, it account for inflation. Return is just (ending total return - beginning total return)/beginning total return. The S&P 500 for example gives 8%-10% a year usually, depends on the time period you look at. It includes dividends/interest AND price appreciation.

The S&P 500 also has a great deal of risk. The worst returns are -40% per year and the best returns are +50% per year. That's a wide range of returns so a very wide standard deviation relative to most other asset classes.

Investors expect a high long-term return as compensation for the variability in yearly returns which is why stonks do so well as an asset class if you zoom out far enough. Real question is should you/could you stomach the volatility?

62367721
>>62367658
All investments take on risk except for HYSA, 3 month US treasuries, and money market funds in theory. Those are the "risk free" rate of return.

With catastrophe bonds you're betting on a lot of different natural disasters not happening in a lot of different places at once.

If you buy regular investment grade bonds you're betting on the US government or big corporations paying you back and not getting fucked by inflation while you wait (reinvestment risk, interest rate risk, default risk).

If you buy stocks you're betting on those companies being profitable in the future (a bajillion different risks).

If you buy real estate you're betting on inflation and low interest rates.

Every investment has inherent risk. The more risk you take on the higher your expected long-term return is since risk = return.

2008 crashed because the Clinton administration coerced banks into giving loans to people that couldn't pay them back. It took a few years to come due but then everyone defaulted on the mortgages they should have never been approved for in the first place and the whole system almost collapsed.

62367729
>>62367683

>8-10%
>great deal of risk
>wide range of returns
you sound like you're just eyeballing this in every case. Do you have some exact set of steps you follow to calculate these? Are your "units of risk and return" actually defined???

>Risk is defined as the uncertainty of future returns. It can be measure by volatility, or how wide the returns are historically. The statistical measure of this is standard deviation.
>Investors expect a high long-term return as compensation for the variability in yearly returns which is why stonks do so well as an asset class if you zoom out far enough. Real question is should you/could you stomach the volatility?
I know... This is 101 and doesn't address my questions at all.

62367855
>>62367729
Not trying to frustrate you man, just wasn't sure what you were exactly asking for. Attached is actual standard deviation calcs I ran on last 26 years of data for different asset classes. These will change depending on time period you look at.

SHARPE ratio is what you want. This measures return per unit of risk. Here's the formula: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sharpe_ratio

You can probably see why I didn't just post the gibberish formula.

You can calculate standard deviation on your own in excel with the STDEV formula on a data set. The reason it changes is because the standard deviation will change depending on the time period you look at.

>you sound like you're just eyeballing this in every case. Do you have some exact set of steps you follow to calculate these? Are your "units of risk and return" actually defined???

I am note. I pull historical returns and calculate historical standard deviation and return then use those to calculate sharpe ratio. You can do it in excel if you get the data.

62367865
>>62367729
im an economist and everything he says there is common knowledge. dyor a bit. he makes good threads by biz standards

I'm an amateur mathematician and CSfag so exact algorithm and I/O is what I was looking for and you got it. How do you decide the timeframe when calculating stddev? Do you just go with other historical periods with similar conditions to present? Do you compare multiple cases like typical years vs. a repeat of 2008 or something? Anything else?

i think you should word on your reading skills if you tyhought that was arrogant. i have RSI iwas typing an answer for you but forget it then

62368159
>>62368104
>How do you decide the timeframe when calculating stddev?
I go as far back as I can as long as the data is reliable with 1972 as a hard stop start year.

1972 because the Nixon shock was '71, which started our current financial system.

As far back as I can go because it captures all the different regimes. Inflation/interest rates mostly. We have been in a low interest rate, low inflation regime for awhile which is NOT normal, it'll change (or already has) soon.

Shorter, near term history is useful too. SOx Act, Dodd-Frank, etc are all relatively recent and changed the regulatory environment. It's a balance of what happened in the past in other regimes way back, what happened recently under current regime/regulation, and then trying to predict what happens next. So
>1972-present
>trailing 30 years
>trailing 10 years

Those are the most useful for me.

>Do you just go with other historical periods with similar conditions to present
Related, I actually want time periods w/ different conditions. I want to know what asset classes did great during the fucked up periods in the 80s when mortgages were 18% and when we had stagflation last (very relevant soon probably). Stocks did balls during that.

>Do you compare multiple cases like typical years vs. a repeat of 2008 or something? Anything else?
Yes, a "typical" year will be 1 standard deviation since inception to measure risk, that's where it gets super useful. You can show a client how much the S&P 500 has gone up & down 1 STD (2/3 of all years) in a given time period and see how much risk they can stomach.

We also show them the worst case years for different portfolios, which are all 2008 or 2022 this regime. Helps them figure out how much drawdown they can take on before vomiting.

62368175
>>62368133
That's a really good way to put it imo, trying to use history to predict the future is the best we have but there's nuance to selecting timeframe. That's why I pick multiple.
